title: Dyckia brevispicata
taxonomy: Dyckia brevispicata is classified as a species of the genus Dyckia, which belongs to the family Bromeliaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants known as bromeliads. The Bromeliaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Dyckia being part of the Pitcairnioideae subfamily. The species Dyckia brevispicata is native to Brazil and is a type of succulent bromeliad, characterized by its thick, waxy leaves and ability to store water. The classification of Dyckia brevispicata is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other bromeliads. The species is relatively rare and is often cultivated by enthusiasts of succulent plants.
